title: Francophone Minority Communities Student Pilot : Participating designated learning institutions and financial requirements - Canada.ca
content:
## Participating designated learning institutions and financial requirements
- How much money you need to participate in the Francophone Minority Communities Student Pilot (FMCSP)
To be eligible for a study permit under this pilot, you must
- apply to a program of study and receive a letter of acceptance from one of the schools on the list below Make sure the school knows that you’re interested in applying for a study permit through the FMCSP. More participating schools may be added to the list below in the future.
- Make sure the school knows that you’re interested in applying for a study permit through the FMCSP.
- More participating schools may be added to the list below in the future.
- meet the financial requirement, which depends on the city where the main campus of your school is located This means you must have the amount of money listed below , in addition to your first year of tuition and travel costs.
- This means you must have the amount of money listed below , in addition to your first year of tuition and travel costs.

## Participating designated learning institutions
As agreements are reached with learning institutions (outside Quebec) to participate in the pilot, they will be added there. Please check the list regularly.
Earliest eligible semester
Université de Saint-Boniface (website available in French only)
Multiple campuses Wellington, Deblois, Charlottetown
Wellington, Deblois, Charlottetown
Prince Edward Island
Université Saint Paul
University of Regina
Collège Éducacentre (website available in French only)
Université Saint-Anne (website available in French only)
University of Ottawa
Université Laurentienne (website available in French only)
Université de l’Ontario français
Université de Hearst (website available in French only)
Multiple campuses Hearst, Kapuskasing, Timmins
Hearst, Kapuskasing, Timmins
University of Alberta
Collège Communautaire du Nouveau-Brunswick (website available in French only)
Multiple campuses Acadian Peninsula, Bathurst, Campbellton, Edmundston, and Dieppe
Acadian Peninsula, Bathurst, Campbellton, Edmundston, and Dieppe
Multiple campuses Sudbury, Toronto, Windsor, Ottawa, Nipissing, Timmins, Hearst, Kapuskasing
Sudbury, Toronto, Windsor, Ottawa, Nipissing, Timmins, Hearst, Kapuskasing
Université de Moncton (website available in French only)
Multiple campuses Edmundston, Moncton and Shippagan
Edmundston, Moncton and Shippagan

## How much money you need
The amount of money you need to have for living expenses to be eligible for the FMCSP depends on the size of the community where the main campus of your school is located. You must also show that you have enough money to pay your tuition fees.

### Group A - Population of 500,000 and over
Number of family members (including the applicant)
Amount of funds required per year (not including tuition)
If more than 7 people, each additional family member

### Group B – Population of 100,000 to 499,999
Number of family members (including the applicant)
Amount of funds required per year (not including tuition)
If more than 7 people, each additional family member

### Group C – Population of 30,000 to 99,999
Number of family members (including the applicant)
Amount of funds required per year (not including tuition)
If more than 7 people, each additional family member

### Group D – Population under 30,000
Number of family members (including the applicant)
Amount of funds required per year (not including tuition)
If more than 7 people, each additional family member

### Group E – Rural areas
Number of family members (including the applicant)
Amount of funds required per year (not including tuition)
If more than 7 people, each additional family member
